symptoms.symptom_name: Severe boring epigastric pain radiating through to the back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Epigastric pain radiating to the back is characteristic of retroperitoneal pancreatic inflammation.
Severe epigastric pain is also consistent with a perforated viscus.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain relieved by leaning forward, worsened by lying supine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positional relief on sitting forward is a well-recognised feature of pancreatitis (and other retroperitoneal processes).
A perforated viscus typically produces peritonism that is worse with any movement, without clear postural relief.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profuse bilious vomiting with inability to tolerate oral fluids

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Repeated vomiting with reactive ileus is common; the inability to tolerate fluids drives the hypovolaemia and the need for IV resuscitation.
Alcohol is a leading cause of acute pancreatitis; a binge immediately preceding onset is a strong aetiological clue.
Low-grade fever fits an early inflammatory response but is non-specific; a high or rigoring fever would raise cholangitis or infected necrosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dehydration signs — weakness, lethargy, dry mucous membranes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Volume depletion from third-spacing plus vomiting; marks severity and the need for IV fluids.
Objective early hypovolaemic shock — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers volume depletion from history (dry mouth, weakness, reduced urine), not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Epigastric tenderness with voluntary guarding and abdominal distension

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised epigastric tenderness supports pancreatitis; generalised board-like rigidity would instead suggest perforation — a distinction requiring hands-on examination.
Reactive paralytic ileus is common in pancreatitis; requires auscultation and so is sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum lipase/amylase greater than three times the upper limit of normal

loinc_ref.display: Lipase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma

investigation_details.expected_result: Greater than three times the upper limit of normal
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis unconfirmed and severity ungraded — cannot be obtained via telehealth, requires venepuncture at hospital.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lipase >3x ULN with consistent pain meets diagnostic criteria; a hospital blood test never available in real time via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: No periumbilical (Cullen's) or flank (Grey Turner's) ecchymosis; sclerae anicteric

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of haemorrhagic signs argues against established necrotising/haemorrhagic pancreatitis at this stage; anicteric sclerae make an obstructing gallstone less likely. Examination-only, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of generalised board-like abdominal rigidity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse rigidity/peritonism would be expected with perforation; its absence lowers that probability but does not exclude it remotely.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Epigastric pain radiating to the back, positional relief on leaning forward, profuse vomiting, and lipase >3x ULN together are highly specific for acute pancreatitis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms pancreatitis when >3x ULN; obtained at hospital — the AI Doctor should arrange ED transfer where this is drawn, not attempt it remotely.
Assesses dehydration and acute kidney injury; guides fluid resuscitation — a hospital blood test.
Excludes an atypical inferior MI presenting as epigastric pain with vomiting; cannot be done via telehealth.
Identifies gallstones as an aetiology and grades severity/complications; a hospital investigation, not first-line at initial contact.
Screens for free air if perforation is being excluded; hospital-based.
